ip_forward_enable.description: Enable IPv4 or IPv6 packet forwarding; use for forwarding/routing enablement, not interface up/down.

ovs_set_controller.description: Set an Open vSwitch bridge controller endpoint.

connectivity_verify.description: Run ping as a verification step or explicit connectivity test.

firewall_allow_pair.description: Allow bidirectional forwarded traffic between two explicit IP endpoints using the FORWARD chain. Use only when the intent describes traffic between a source endpoint and a destination endpoint, and both src_ip and dst_ip are available. Do not use for allowing traffic on a router interface, traffic destined to the router itself, service-specific INPUT rules, ICMP echo requests on an interface, or generic ACL permit rules on a router; use firewall_input_accept or firewall_output_accept instead.
templates: iptables -C FORWARD -s {src_ip} -d {dst_ip} -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || iptables -I FORWARD -s {src_ip} -d {dst_ip} -j ACCEPT
iptables -C FORWARD -s {dst_ip} -d {src_ip} -j ACCEPT 2>/dev/null || iptables -I FORWARD -s {dst_ip} -d {src_ip} -j ACCEPT

qos_prioritize_pair.description: Prioritize traffic from one host to another using iptables mark and tc.
templates: iptables -t mangle -C OUTPUT -d {dst_ip} -j MARK --set-mark {mark} 2>/dev/null || iptables -t mangle -A OUTPUT -d {dst_ip} -j MARK --set-mark {mark}
tc qdisc replace dev {src_host}-eth0 root handle 1: prio
tc filter replace dev {src_host}-eth0 parent 1: protocol ip prio 1 handle {mark} fw flowid 1:1

firewall_drop_subnet_pair.description: Drop forwarded traffic between explicit source and destination CIDR blocks. Use only when both a source CIDR and a destination CIDR/subnet are explicitly available or intentionally derived as CIDR filters. Do not use for host-to-router-LAN intents that should filter by ingress or egress interface; use firewall_forward_drop instead.
templates: iptables -C FORWARD -s {src_cidr} -d {dst_cidr} -j DROP 2>/dev/null || iptables -A FORWARD -s {src_cidr} -d {dst_cidr} -j DROP

interface_l3_address_config.description: Configure IPv4 or IPv6 address on an interface; do not use for MTU, description, or up/down.

iface_mac_config.description: Set the MAC address of an interface.

logging_config.description: Configure FRR logging target and optional log level.

static_route_blackhole_add.description: Add an IPv4 blackhole route to Null0; use when intent says blackhole, discard, or Null0. Only use this when the intent explicitly says blackhole, discard, drop route, or Null0; do not use for ordinary static routes with a next-hop.

static_route_add_frr.description: Add/install/create/configure an IPv4 static route in FRR using a next-hop IP. Administrative distance is optional and must only be used when explicitly requested by the intent, such as distance, administrative distance, backup route, floating route, or preference.

arp_static_entry_config.description: Create a static ARP entry mapping target IP to MAC on an interface.
templates: ip neigh add {target_ip} lladdr {mac_address} dev {interface} nud permanent

ipv6_forward_enable.description: Enable IPv6 forwarding only; prefer ip_forward_enable when choosing by address_family.

static_route_add_ipv6_frr.description: Add/install/create/configure an IPv6 static route in FRR using either a next-hop IP or an exit interface. Use next_hop_ip when the intent provides or derives a next-hop address. Use exit_interface when the intent explicitly routes through an interface. Administrative distance is optional and must only be used when explicitly requested by the intent, such as distance, administrative distance, backup route, floating route, or preference.

bgp_peer_group_config.description: Create/configure an explicitly named BGP peer-group and set its remote AS. Use only when the intent explicitly mentions a peer-group/group name. Do not use for a normal BGP neighbor relationship.

bgp_neighbor_peer_group_bind.description: Bind a BGP neighbor IP to an explicitly existing or configured peer-group. Use only when the intent explicitly asks to bind/attach/associate a neighbor to a peer-group. Do not use for direct BGP peering.

bgp_neighbor_med_set.description: Set outbound MED for a BGP neighbor using a route-map.
templates: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'route-map {route_map_name} permit 10' -c 'set metric {med}' -c 'router bgp {local_as}' -c 'neighbor {neighbor_ip} route-map {route_map_name} out'

bgp_as_path_multipath_enable.description: Enable BGP AS-path multipath relax under a local BGP process.
templates: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp {local_as}' -c 'bgp bestpath as-path multipath-relax'

arp_encapsulation_set.description: Enable or disable ARP encapsulation/state on an interface; not for static ARP entries.

interface_admin_config.description: DEPRECATED: do not select this operation. Use interface_admin_config with enabled=true for interface up/no shutdown intents.

interface_attribute_config.description: Configure FRR-level interface attributes such as description. Do not use for Linux interface MTU, administrative state, MAC address, or IP address. For MTU in Mininet/Linux, use interface_mtu_kernel.

arp_static_entry_del.description: Delete a static ARP entry for a target IP on a specific interface.

interface_mtu_kernel.description: Set the MTU of an interface at the kernel level using iproute2. Use this for actual MTU enforcement in Mininet/Linux; interface_attribute_config sets MTU only in FRR's view.

static_route_del_frr.description: Remove an IPv4 static route from FRR by destination prefix, with optional next-hop and distance for precision. Use for delete/remove route intents targeting FRR routing table. Only use this when the intent explicitly says delete, remove, withdraw, or no route; do not use for ordinary add/configure static route intents.

interface_l3_address_remove.description: Remove an IPv4 or IPv6 address from an interface in FRR.

interface_attribute_remove.description: Remove an interface attribute such as description in FRR. Use attribute_type='description' to clear a description.

firewall_input_drop.description: Drop traffic on the INPUT chain. Use when blocking traffic on a router itself, traffic destined to the router, or intents phrased as 'block ... on router X' without mentioning forwarding, passing through, another LAN, ingress/egress path, or routed traffic. Supports protocol, destination port, source IP/CIDR, and ingress interface.

firewall_forward_drop.description: Drop forwarded traffic using the FORWARD chain. Use only when the intent explicitly describes traffic passing through the router, being forwarded/routed between networks, reaching/leaving another router LAN, or using an ingress/egress interface. Do not use for traffic destined to the router itself, INPUT-chain permits, ICMP echo requests on a router interface, or generic ACL permit rules.

firewall_input_accept.description: Accept traffic on the router INPUT chain. Use when the intent permits/allows traffic destined to the router itself or arriving on a router interface, including LAN-facing interfaces. Supports protocol, destination port, source IP/CIDR, ICMP type, and ingress interface. For ICMP echo requests on a router interface, use protocol='icmp', icmp_type='echo-request', and in_interface. Do not use for bidirectional host-to-host or endpoint-pair forwarding; use firewall_allow_pair only when both source and destination endpoints are part of the intended forwarded traffic relation.

firewall_output_drop.description: Drop traffic on the OUTPUT chain. Use for outbound ACLs on a router, especially when blocking locally originated service traffic such as telnet, SSH, HTTP, or HTTPS. Supports protocol and destination port.

firewall_output_accept.description: Accept traffic on the OUTPUT chain by protocol and optional destination port or egress interface.

tc_egress_rate_limit.description: Apply an HTB egress rate limit on an interface for all traffic or filtered by protocol/port. Generates qdisc + class + filter commands.

templates: tc qdisc add dev {interface} root handle {handle} htb default {default_class}
tc class add dev {interface} parent {handle} classid {classid} htb rate {rate_mbit}mbit
tc filter add dev {interface} parent {handle} protocol ip u32 match ip protocol {ip_proto_num} 0xff flowid {classid}
tc qdisc add dev {interface} root handle {handle} htb default {default_class}
tc class add dev {interface} parent {handle} classid {classid} htb rate {rate_mbit}mbit
tc filter add dev {interface} parent {handle} protocol ip u32 match ip dport {dst_port} 0xffff flowid {classid}
tc qdisc add dev {interface} root handle {handle} htb default {default_class}
tc class add dev {interface} parent {handle} classid {classid} htb rate {rate_mbit}mbit
tc filter add dev {interface} parent {handle} protocol ip u32 match ip dst 0.0.0.0/0 flowid {classid}

tc_ingress_police.description: Police inbound traffic on an interface using tc ingress + filter. Supports filtering by protocol number. Use for inbound rate limiting.

templates: tc filter add dev {interface} parent ffff: protocol ip u32 match ip protocol {ip_proto_num} 0xff police rate {rate_mbit}mbit burst {burst} drop flowid :1
tc filter add dev {interface} parent ffff: protocol ip u32 match ip src 0.0.0.0/0 police rate {rate_mbit}mbit burst {burst} drop flowid :1

tc_priority_by_port.description: Apply prio qdisc and direct traffic matching a destination port to the highest-priority band. Use for SSH/service prioritization on an interface.
templates: tc qdisc add dev {interface} root handle {handle} prio bands {bands}
tc filter add dev {interface} parent {handle} protocol ip u32 match ip dport {dst_port} 0xffff flowid {flowid}

bgp_neighbor_config.description: Configure a direct BGP neighbor with remote-as, without using a peer-group. Use when the intent asks to peer with a router/device or neighbor directly, whether the neighbor IP is explicit or must be derived from topology.

bgp_network_advertise.description: Advertise a network prefix into BGP. The prefix must exist in the routing table or be redistributed.

bgp_neighbor_remove.description: Remove a BGP neighbor by IP address from a BGP process.

bgp_peer_group_remove.description: Remove a BGP peer-group from a BGP process. All neighbors bound to the group must be unbound first or will be removed implicitly.

bgp_local_preference_set.description: Set BGP local-preference for a neighbor or peer-group using a route-map applied inbound. Route-map is defined and applied in a single vtysh call.

bgp_route_map_apply.description: Apply an existing named route-map to a BGP neighbor in a given direction (in/out). Use when the route-map already exists and only the binding needs to be set.

ospf_interface_cost.description: Set the OSPF cost (metric) of an interface under FRR.
